blockers.title: Monitor A and Monitor B verdict files both absent
blockers.requirement: S8 protocol requires all 3 agent verdicts before S9. Bootstrap protocol §1 S8: '3 agent 全 ACCEPT → S9'
blockers.risk: S9 GitHub publish without Agent A/B sign-off violates the 3-agent gate. Cannot determine consensus.

blockers.title: top1_vertex_accuracy = 0.000 and top5_vertex_accuracy = 0.000 across ALL 7 ablation settings
blockers.evidence: cat results/v0.1.0a1_ablation.json → metrics_by_setting: every setting has top1=0.0, top5=0.0
blockers.detail: run_ablation.py lines 98-109: vertex edge matching (edge[2]==target_a AND edge[3]==target_b) never hits for any of the 30 synthetic ground truth rows across all 7 settings. This is either (a) a structural mismatch between how synthetic pairs are indexed vs how vertex edges are keyed, or (b) the polytope vertex extraction emits edges in a different index space than the ground truth feature_id_A/feature_id_B fields.
blockers.impact: README table shows 0.000 / 0.000 for all top-k accuracy cells. While these ARE the real measured values (honest per CLAIM.md), a non-zero rate should be achievable on synthetic data created specifically to match the algorithm's output space. Zero hit rate across all settings is a strong signal of a logic bug, not just low performance.
blockers.risk: Ship-and-yank loop risk (feedback_ship-and-yank-lesson-2026-05-23). This pattern mirrors saelet v0.1.0 placeholder issue.
blockers.note: CLAIM.md correctly tags these as non-CLAIM for real data. But zero synthetic top-k accuracy suggests the ablation logic is broken, not merely uninformative.

blockers.title: CLAIM.md [CLAIM]/[non-CLAIM] tag format does not satisfy HONEST-PREALPHA-COMPLETE CI grep
blockers.evidence: grep -E '^\[(CLAIM|non-CLAIM)\]' docs/CLAIM.md → 0 lines. Tags are inline (e.g. '- `[CLAIM]` text...') not line-leading.
blockers.requirement: Bootstrap protocol §7 item 4: 'grep -E "^\[(CLAIM|non-CLAIM)\]" docs/CLAIM.md 全行該当'
blockers.impact: The CI check as specified would fail or return 0 lines, making the HONEST-PREALPHA-COMPLETE checkbox unmeetable as written. Either the grep in §7 needs adjustment (prefix after '- ') or CLAIM.md format needs to be changed to line-leading tags.
blockers.risk: Low functional risk (content is honest), but the mechanical check gate fails.

nits.title: audit.yml does not use astral-sh/setup-uv (no uv install step)
nits.evidence: grep -E 'enable-cache|setup-uv' .github/workflows/audit.yml → no match. audit.yml uses only actions/checkout@v4 + inline bash.
nits.assessment: audit.yml jobs (banned-phrase, demo-prefix, diff-disclaimer, vacuous-test) are pure grep/bash and do not need uv. This is acceptable. The ci.yml correctly uses astral-sh/setup-uv@v3 with enable-cache:true. Non-blocking.

nits.title: dependabot.yml does not explicitly enable security-updates
nits.evidence: cat .github/dependabot.yml → version-updates with limit:0 for both pip and github-actions ecosystems. No separate security-updates stanza.
nits.assessment: GitHub enables Dependabot security alerts by default for public repos. The open-pull-requests-limit:0 applies to version-updates only; security alerts are repo-level settings, not controlled via dependabot.yml. Per feedback_github_org_hygiene_2026-05-25, security PRs can be admin-merged. Non-blocking but worth explicit confirmation post-S9.

nits.title: Single commit history — all work in one commit
nits.evidence: git log --oneline | head -10 → only '97b1200 polyalign v0.1.0a1: initial pre-alpha scaffold'
nits.assessment: Single squash commit is acceptable for pre-alpha. No banned phrases detected in the message. Non-blocking.

nits.title: ECE values ~0.999 on synthetic data require disclaimer
nits.evidence: ablation.json: ece ranges 0.9983-0.9997 across all settings. This is expected for synthetic data where all predictions cluster near one extreme.
nits.assessment: CLAIM.md correctly scopes ECE as calibration shape only (non-CLAIM for concept fidelity). However README should note that ECE near 1.0 on 5-vertex synthetic output is expected behavior, not a calibration defect. Non-blocking but reviewers may misread.

consensus_summary: Monitor A and Monitor B verdict files are absent (OTHERS-PENDING). Agent C completed its pre-flight checks independently. Two HIGH blockers identified: (1) A/B verdicts missing — 3-agent gate is structurally violated; (2) top1/top5 vertex accuracy = 0.000 across all 7 ablation settings, indicating a likely logic bug in the synthetic top-k matching code (run_ablation.py lines 95-109). One HIGH-severity format blocker on CLAIM.md line-leading tag grep. GitHub pre-flight items that can be verified pre-S9 all pass: name available, PyPI absent, gh auth OK, git remote unset, ci.yml correct (setup-uv@v3 + enable-cache:true), dependabot.yml version-updates suppressed, no greet workflow, no banned commit phrases.
verdict_text: DO-NOT-SHIP. Two independent blockers prevent S9: (1) Monitor A and B verdict files absent after 3-minute wait — the 3-agent gate is not satisfied. (2) top1 and top5 vertex accuracy are 0.000 across all 7 ablation settings in results/v0.1.0a1_ablation.json, indicating a likely index-space mismatch bug in run_ablation.py lines 95-109 (synthetic feature_id → vertex edge lookup never matches). Fix the accuracy logic and re-run ablation; re-trigger Monitor A and B; only then re-run Agent C for final gate clearance.
next_steps: Diagnose and fix top-k accuracy logic in scripts/run_ablation.py (lines 95-109): verify that synthetic ground truth feature_id_A/B values are in the same index space as vertex.model_pairs edge tuples.
Re-run ablation runner and verify non-zero top1 or top5 rate on synthetic data (a synthetic dataset designed to match the algorithm should achieve >0.0).
Fix CLAIM.md tag format: either change to line-leading '[CLAIM] ...' format or update the §7 grep to match '- `[CLAIM]`' inline format.
Wait for / trigger Monitor A (Builder) and Monitor B (Critic) to produce their verdict files.
Re-run Agent C after A+B verdicts are present and blockers C-B2/C-B3 are resolved.
